Title: Lead AI & Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you enjoy developing and expanding AI/ML solutions that deliver measurable business results? Do you enjoy working at the intersection of product, engineering and business to turn advanced AI capabilities into production-ready solutions? Then you might be our new AI/ML Engineer Specialist.

Digital Solutions & Development > Digital Solutions > AI/ML

As part of the Digital Development Platform, the AI/ML Chapter is the home base for AI/ML specialization and supports Engineering Excellence, capability building, and scalable delivery throughout Vestas. You will be part of a global team spanning Denmark and India, partnering closely with product owners, AI/ML engineers and business stakeholders to design, build, and scale AI/ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions across the value chain from R&D and manufacturing to supply chain and service.

The chapter focuses on building and scaling AI/ML capabilities for high-value and autonomous flow use cases. It maintains alignment across product areas, keeps the organization prepared for emerging paradigms like LLMs and agentic AI, and supports scalable delivery through a balanced approach of internal development and strategic partnerships. With disciplined engineering practices, a data-centered approach, and close collaboration with business partners, the team consistently raises AI/ML delivery standards and supports solutions that improve visibility, optimization, and innovation across the enterprise.

As part of the broader Autonomous Flows initiative, the team contributes to building next-generation intelligent systems and agentic workflows that enhance operational efficiency and unlock new business value. We collaborate closely with platform, product and governance teams to ensure scalable, responsible, and impactful adoption of AI across Vestas.

Responsibilities

As a senior member of the product team, you will collaborate with product owners, AI/ML engineers, developers and business stakeholders to build and scale AI/ML capabilities in digital products. The position calls for collaborative informal leadership, with an emphasis on speeding up delivery and increasing business impact.


Lead the design, delivery, and scaling of ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions to generate measurable business impact across the Vestas value chain
Drive the integration of AI and ML capabilities into enterprise applications to support smooth adoption and measurable value
Shape architecture choices that enable AI/ML solutions to scale reliably and ensure maintainability over its lifecyle
Establish and promote reusable patterns and engineering standards for scalable delivery, AI-ready data, and trusted AI automation
Own the end-to-end delivery across multiple initiatives, balancing speed, quality and evolving priorities
Guide stakeholders on AI/ML strategy, shaping solution design to align with business objectives and governance requirements


Qualifications

You have a solid background in machine learning and software engineering, along with effective communication and stakeholder collaboration skills. You bring substantial experience in creating and scaling AI/ML systems for production, including end-to-end ownership from defining the problem to launching and iterating on the solution. You are recognized for your depth in one or more areas such as NLP/GenAI/Agentic systems, computer vision, ML engineering or large-scale AI systems. We hope that you are able to recognize yourself in the following:


A master's degree in a relevant field (e.g., computer science, data science, engineering) or equivalent practical experience, combined with approximately 10 years of experience in ML engineering, designing, delivering, and scaling production-grade AI/ML solutions
Advanced proficiency in Python and production-level software engineering, with practical experience using ML frameworks such as Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and PyTorch, GenAI/agentic frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, and Pydantic AI, and AI-assisted development tools such as GitHub Copilot and Claude Code to speed up delivery and support innovation
Demonstrated depth in AI experimentation and modeling, with experience designing robust, scalable training and inference pipelines on cloud platforms such as Azure and Snowflake, along with CI/CD deployment in IaC environment
Demonstrated ability to lead end-to-end delivery of AI/ML solutions, make architecture decisions, and integrate enterprise systems through APIs, microservices, Docker-based containerization, Kubernetes deployment, and scalable, production-ready engineering practices
Extensive experience in MLOps and scalable AI/ML systems and deployment engineering, with work spanning ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions in production; tuning reliability, performance, latency, throughput, and cost; and establishing governance, monitoring, observability, automated retraining, versioning, and data-drift detection across AI/ML environments


Competencies

As our team is globally founded and very collaborative, we hope you will be part of the mutual enablement we emphasize. We believe that a way to do so it through:


Effective collaboration among teams spread across Denmark, India, and Portugal, with heightened cross-cultural awareness and clear communication
Skilled at bringing product, AI/ML engineering, platform, and MLOps teams together around shared engineering outcomes
Demonstrated skill in mentoring and empowering teams through knowledge sharing, best practices, and informal leadership
Demonstrated ability to align AI architecture and development standards, including building agreement in technical forums with enterprise architects and technical leads


What We Offer


Purpose. An opportunity to help address climate change and shape the future for generations to come by building AI/ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions that advance renewable energy and sustainable operations
Accelerated learning and growth. Join an accomplished global community of AI/ML engineers, data scientists, and GenAI professionals, where continuous learning, knowledge exchange, and expansion into agentic autonomous flows are central priorities
Global collaboration and a supportive team culture. Work within a multicultural Denmark-India setup that values trust, collaboration and mutual support to deliver meaningful impact
Opportunity to shape your role. Take a central part in expanding agentic autonomous workflows and high-value AI/ML solutions, with freedom to guide direction, methods, and results
Supportive and inclusive environment. Thrive in a diverse, inclusive workplace that promotes well-being, work-life balance and enables individuals to bring their full potential to work
Career growth in a global organization. Become part of a worldwide organization where you can keep building your abilities and move your career forward


Overall, we provide an opportunity to contribute meaningfully while advancing your career in a global, multicultural environment that supports ongoing learning and development.

Additional Information

Location: Aarhus or Copenhagen, Denmark

Application Deadline: July 28th, 2026. We will review applications on an ongoing basis. We can withdraw from the job and reserve the right to do so at any time, including before the advertised closing date.

Start date: September 1st, 2026. Based on your fit and requirements, we can make adjustments as needed.

Our commitment to a fair hiring.

At Vestas, we evaluate all candidates solely based on their professional experience, education, and relevant skills. To support a fair recruitment process, we kindly ask that you remove any photos, dates of birth or graduation, gender pronouns, marital status, or other personal information not relevant to the role before submitting your CV/resume. Your CV/resume should focus on your professional and educational background, along with the necessary contact details (email and phone number). We train our hiring teams in inclusive evaluation and regularly review process outcomes to ensure fairness.

DEIB Statement

At Vestas, we recognize the value of diversity, equity, and inclusion in driving innovation and success. We strongly encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply, particularly those who may hesitate due to their identity or feel they do not meet every criterion. As our CEO states, "Expertise and talent come in many forms, and a diverse workforce enhances our ability to think differently and solve the complex challenges of our industry". Your unique perspective is what will help us powering the solution for a sustainable, green energy future.

BEWARE – RECRUITMENT FRAUD

It has come to our attention that there are a number of fraudulent emails from people pretending to work for Vestas. Read more via this link, https://www.vestas.com/en/careers/our-recruitment-process

About Vestas

Vestas is the energy industry’s global partner on sustainable energy solutions. We are specialised in designing, manufacturing, installing, and servicing wind turbines, both onshore and offshore.

Across the globe, we have installed more wind power than anyone else. We consider ourselves pioneers within the industry, as we continuously aim to design new solutions and technologies to create a more sustainable future for all of us. With more than 200 GW of wind power installed worldwide and 40+ years of experience in wind energy, we have an unmatched track record demonstrating our expertise within the field.

With 39,000 employees globally, we are a diverse team united by a common goal: to power the solution – today, tomorrow, and far into the future.

Vestas promotes a diverse workforce which embraces all social identities and is free of any discrimination. We commit to create and sustain an environment that acknowledges and harvests different experiences, skills, and perspectives. We also aim to give everyone equal access to opportunity.

To learn more about our company and life at Vestas, we invite you to visit our website at www.vestas.com and follow us on our social media channels. We also encourage you to join our Talent Universe to receive notifications on new and relevant postings.

Do you enjoy developing and expanding AI/ML solutions that deliver measurable business results? Do you enjoy working at the intersection of product, engineering and business to turn advanced AI capabilities into production-ready solutions? Then you might be our new AI/ML Engineer Specialist.

Digital Solutions & Development > Digital Solutions > AI/ML

As part of the Digital Development Platform, the AI/ML Chapter is the home base for AI/ML specialization and supports Engineering Excellence, capability building, and scalable delivery throughout Vestas. You will be part of a global team spanning Denmark and India, partnering closely with product owners, AI/ML engineers and business stakeholders to design, build, and scale AI/ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions across the value chain from R&D and manufacturing to supply chain and service.

The chapter focuses on building and scaling AI/ML capabilities for high-value and autonomous flow use cases. It maintains alignment across product areas, keeps the organization prepared for emerging paradigms like LLMs and agentic AI, and supports scalable delivery through a balanced approach of internal development and strategic partnerships. With disciplined engineering practices, a data-centered approach, and close collaboration with business partners, the team consistently raises AI/ML delivery standards and supports solutions that improve visibility, optimization, and innovation across the enterprise.

As part of the broader Autonomous Flows initiative, the team contributes to building next-generation intelligent systems and agentic workflows that enhance operational efficiency and unlock new business value. We collaborate closely with platform, product and governance teams to ensure scalable, responsible, and impactful adoption of AI across Vestas.

Responsibilities

As a senior member of the product team, you will collaborate with product owners, AI/ML engineers, developers and business stakeholders to build and scale AI/ML capabilities in digital products. The position calls for collaborative informal leadership, with an emphasis on speeding up delivery and increasing business impact.

  • Lead the design, delivery, and scaling of ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions to generate measurable business impact across the Vestas value chain
  • Drive the integration of AI and ML capabilities into enterprise applications to support smooth adoption and measurable value
  • Shape architecture choices that enable AI/ML solutions to scale reliably and ensure maintainability over its lifecyle
  • Establish and promote reusable patterns and engineering standards for scalable delivery, AI-ready data, and trusted AI automation
  • Own the end-to-end delivery across multiple initiatives, balancing speed, quality and evolving priorities
  • Guide stakeholders on AI/ML strategy, shaping solution design to align with business objectives and governance requirements

Qualifications

You have a solid background in machine learning and software engineering, along with effective communication and stakeholder collaboration skills. You bring substantial experience in creating and scaling AI/ML systems for production, including end-to-end ownership from defining the problem to launching and iterating on the solution. You are recognized for your depth in one or more areas such as NLP/GenAI/Agentic systems, computer vision, ML engineering or large-scale AI systems. We hope that you are able to recognize yourself in the following:

  • A master's degree in a relevant field (e.g., computer science, data science, engineering) or equivalent practical experience, combined with approximately 10 years of experience in ML engineering, designing, delivering, and scaling production-grade AI/ML solutions
  • Advanced proficiency in Python and production-level software engineering, with practical experience using ML frameworks such as Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and PyTorch, GenAI/agentic frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, and Pydantic AI, and AI-assisted development tools such as GitHub Copilot and Claude Code to speed up delivery and support innovation
  • Demonstrated depth in AI experimentation and modeling, with experience designing robust, scalable training and inference pipelines on cloud platforms such as Azure and Snowflake, along with CI/CD deployment in IaC environment
  • Demonstrated ability to lead end-to-end delivery of AI/ML solutions, make architecture decisions, and integrate enterprise systems through APIs, microservices, Docker-based containerization, Kubernetes deployment, and scalable, production-ready engineering practices
  • Extensive experience in MLOps and scalable AI/ML systems and deployment engineering, with work spanning ML, GenAI, and agentic solutions in production; tuning reliability, performance, latency, throughput, and cost; and establishing governance, monitoring, observability, automated retraining, versioning, and data-drift detection across AI/ML environments

Title: Founding Data/ML Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Who we’re looking for 👀

We're looking for a data/ML engineer to own the brain of Palette: the signal engine.

The ideal person sits at the intersection of four hats:


Data engineer: owns the pipelines at real volume, and takes data privacy and security seriously
ML engineer: embeddings, clustering, retrieval, vector search, LLMs as system components, evals
Data scientist: spots the use cases and drives what we build next on top of the data
Software engineer: comfortable across the stack, comfortable in TypeScript / Postgres


You won't be equally deep in all four - nobody is. But you're genuinely strong in at least one and eager to grow into the rest. Right skills, but more importantly the right mindset: strong product sense, agency, curiosity. You think in relationships and retrieval, and you care about cost and quality. Bonus points for RAG and graph-shaped data.

At Palette you'll drive our signal engine, end to end. Today it captures events and stores them as signals - enough for the briefs and context pages we ship now. Next is a queryable graph of activities: everything that happens across a company's tools, on one timeline, answerable by who, what, when, and meaning, by both people and AI agents.

You work AI-native - use the tools, move fast, own the output. If the AI writes 80% of the code, great; just reason about it and ship.

The stack 🚀


Desktop: Tauri v2, Rust, TypeScript / React / TanStack
Frontend: Next.js (migrating to TanStack)
Backend: Hono
AI Agents: Mastra, plus the coding-agent harnesses we run on-device
Data & infra: PostgreSQL, Redis, Inngest, Nango, Railway
Plus: Linear, GitHub Actions, WorkOS, Cloudflare, Sentry, Incident.io, Requesty, Swarmia, PostHog, Atlas and more.


We're bullish on TypeScript but we're constantly evaluating our approach and open to be challenged.

What you get 💰


Top-tier salary + warrants
Solid office in Copenhagen
The gear you need
Dental insurance
Lunch, snacks & drinks at the office


How we work 🤝


Copenhagen office. Mostly in person, but around two days from home every week.
We aim to stay a small and lean team.
We're all doers and care about our craftsmanship.
We're constantly exploring what AI-native means to us.
We're obsessed with making something people actually want to use, therefore everyone at the company talks to customers regularly.


Who we are 🎨

For the last decade, knowledge work drifted away from its original promise. It was supposed to be about thinking, creating, deciding. Instead it became status meetings, reporting, alignment rituals, and busywork.

AI changes that. But the speed has created a new problem: the individual got faster than the organization. More and more work happens inside sessions between humans and agents, and the context behind that work disappears before anyone else in the company can see it.

Palette is the shared context layer for teams and AI. We ingest signals from the tools teams already use (Slack, Linear, Notion, GitHub, calendar, email), turn them into a living map of the organization, and serve that context to the agents people work with every day.

Palette Desktop is the app where teams put that context to work. Point it at a folder your team shares on Google Drive or Dropbox, and run agents on Anthropic, OpenAI, Mistral, or a local model inside it. More providers ship in regularly. It opens those agents up to the whole company, including non-engineers, and the team picks the model that fits. We shipped Desktop in May, and it's already where most of our own work happens.

We're 6 people in Copenhagen. Four co-founders, two founding engineers. We work with design partners across leading startups, scaleups, and household-name brands. We recently raised our pre-seed and are now bringing on our first commercial hire.

Please note that during week 29-31 there might be slower response times due to our small team taking some vacation ☀️

Title: Senior Data Scientist - Store Partnering
Place: Albertslund, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Senior Data Scientist – Store Partnering

Coop Danmark, Albertslund (Possibility of remote work)

Do you want to be a key driver in establishing an ambitious new team where decisions are founded in thorough data analysis and where machine learning forecasting methodologies are at the heart of the roadmap? Then this is your opportunity to make your mark on the future of the supply chain at Coop!

Your new team

In the role of Senior Data Scientist you will become a central part of the new Store Partnering team. The team will be heavily focused on spearheading a new approach to data centricity in Coop, and as the team is newly formed, you will be highly able to influence ways of working.

About The Position

As a Senior Data Scientist in Coop’s new Store Partnering team, you will have a central responsibility for establishing and developing the data-driven approach to supply chain processes and decisions. Your focus will be on driving the digital agenda forward in Coop’s supply chain, with the aim of improving the quality and efficiency of planning processes. Focus will be on leveraging the vast data landscape available to a large retail organization - turning data into valuable forecasting and planning action. You will work to make technology, data science and AI a pivotal component in how we do planning to unlock efficiencies in planning processes and improvements to consumer availability for millions of consumers every day.

Your Primary Responsibilities


Tools: Developing promotion of quantity estimation methodologies and related tools. Develop the analytics toolbox that makes campaign and store planning tasks faster to execute.
Data driven culture and problem solving: Drive the agenda of putting analysis, math and statistics behind every decision by supporting the store and demand planning team with advanced analysis for operational decisions and development initiatives.
Measurement & Learning: Help establish the KPI and reporting framework, such that continuous development enables and learnings from campaigns and operations can be reused.
RELEX AI: Driving the ML/AI agenda in Relex and operationalizing relevant large data and AI agent use cases. Includes design, diagnostics, and roadmap development.
Guidance for analysts and specialists: Coaching and sparring with the cohort of analysts and specialists across supply chain planning.


About You

You have solid experience with complex analysis, and you thrive on translating data into concrete improvements. You are analytically strong, structured, and have a natural ability to collaborate across disciplines. You enjoy continuously developing ways of working and are energized about the opportunity to be part of shaping up a new team with a big organizational focus. You are driven by creating structure and momentum in complex deliverables and thrive at the intersection of business, IT, and operations. You can communicate complex statistical and technical concepts and findings for the broader organization.

We imagine that you have several of the following competencies:


4–6 years of experience with applied ML in production environment, preferably within a supply chain or retail context.
Master or PHD within mathematical fields
Strong acumen in data science programming language like Python, R or similar data science programming language and experience with MLOps – model versioning, backtesting, and retraining pipelines
Experienced in feature engineering on demand data, including causal factors such as pricing, promotions, events, and weather
Able to translate complex models into business value and communicate technical results to non-technical stakeholders
Has experience with Relex or similar advanced planning systems


Coop Offers

An exciting role in a highly ambitious environment where you will have the opportunity to make a real difference. You will become part of a dedicated team with a lot of hunger and high professional expertise and strong collaboration, where you help shape the digital agenda in Coop’s supply chain.


Influence on projects that impact our 900 stores and 38,000 colleagues
An attractive compensation package including pension, health insurance, and employee benefits
Discounts in Coop's stores, summer cottage rentals, staff clubs, and purchasing schemes
Flexible working hours and the option of one work-from-home day per week


Your base will be at Coop's head office in Albertslund, close to the teams you collaborate with on a daily basis.

Interested?

Are you ready to help shape the future of data-driven decision-making at one of Scandinavia's largest retailers? Then send your CV via our online application form no later than 03-08-2026. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis, so don't hesitate to apply.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact Supply Chain Manager Bo Bøgvad by email at bo.boegvad@coop.dk

You can read more about Coop as a workplace at www.coop.dk.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Sr Machine Learning / AI Engineer / MLOps Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About The Team

Sitting at the critical intersection of ML engineering, platform engineering, and observability, the Peakon MLOps Engineer serves as the central operational link between development and production. You will work closely with ML engineers, backend engineers, and the central Agent Forge / ML Runtime platform teams to ensure our autonomous agents run reliably and execute complex workflows seamlessly for customers, providing clear runbooks, robust observability, on-call and predictable incident response. Your primary objective is to enable ML engineers to safely ship agent behavior changes and understand their impact by providing streamlined tooling, agent-specific monitoring, and operational support.

About The Role

The Peakon MLOps Engineer is responsible for operating, hardening, and continuously improving the production infrastructure that powers the Peakon Agent, multi-agent architectures, AI Features and related ML workloads. This ownership spans deployments, monitoring, and on-call workflows. Key operational functions include managing the entire deployment lifecycle for the Peakon Agent and other AI Features, ensuring the reliability of long-running agentic loops, memory stores, and tool-use environments, while contributing to driving operational excellence across the ML platform. Furthermore, the role involves building and maintaining tooling to surface agent trajectory and evaluation data, supporting performance testing, latency benchmarking, and load simulations specific to LLM-driven applications, and collaborating on the automation of essential security upgrades for ML dependencies.

About You

Basic Qualifications Summary


Minimum 8 years of relevant industry experience. Holds a Bachelor’s/Master or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
Proven track record as an MLOps or ML-savvy SRE/Platform Engineer supporting production-grade LLM and agentic systems. Proficient in Python and frameworks like LangChain and LlamaIndex.
Hands-on experience operating containerized services (Docker, Kubernetes) using Git-based workflows (GitOps, GitHub Actions). Solid understanding of modern ML stacks (platforms, feature stores, registries, messaging layers) or deep platform engineering background.
Demonstrated ability to own production infrastructure end-to-end—managing monitoring, incident response, rollbacks, and continuous reliability/uptime improvements.
Deep understanding of the model development lifecycle, specifically regarding model monitoring, regression tracking, and automated evaluation using tools like LangSmith.
Strong communication and collaboration skills under pressure, acting as a bridge between ML engineers, backend teams, and central platform/security specialists.


Other Qualifications Summary


Solid knowledge of data science principles and ML algorithms applied directly to LLMs, RAG, and autonomous decision-making agents.
Experience leading model-building processes, including advanced fine-tuning, alignment techniques, prompt engineering, and simulations of agent behaviors.
Strong understanding of software development principles coupled with demonstrated proficiency in System Design and Architectural Governance to deploy, scale, and maintain high-availability ML models.
Expertise in threat modeling and security for ML/agent systems to enforce strict behavioral guardrails. Proven experience navigating highly regulated enterprise environments to ensure data auditability, clear ownership boundaries, and strict compliance.
Track record of strong technical decision quality and leadership, with the ability to coordinate cross-functional initiatives, translate complex business needs into resilient implementations, and mentor team members while bridging the gap between ML engineering and platform teams.


Workday Pay Transparency Statement (For EU Locations Only)

Listed below is the base salary range applicable to this position. Workday pay ranges (and the precise pay offered to the successful candidate) are based on a number of objective criteria such as relevant experience and skills, and educational qualifications, level of responsibility, demands of the role, work location and business need. As a part of the total compensation package, this role may be eligible for the Workday Bonus Plan or a role-specific commission/bonus, as well as annual refresh stock grants awarded by Workday Inc. For more information regarding Workday’s comprehensive benefits, please click here.

Primary Location Base Pay Range: kr838,000 DKK - kr1,256,000 DKK Denmark

Our Approach to Flexible Work

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About The Team

Sitting at the critical intersection of ML engineering, platform engineering, and observability, the Peakon MLOps Engineer serves as the central operational link between development and production. You will work closely with ML engineers, backend engineers, and the central Agent Forge / ML Runtime platform teams to ensure our autonomous agents run reliably and execute complex workflows seamlessly for customers, providing clear runbooks, robust observability, on-call and predictable incident response. Your primary objective is to enable ML engineers to safely ship agent behavior changes and understand their impact by providing streamlined tooling, agent-specific monitoring, and operational support.

About The Role

The Peakon MLOps Engineer is responsible for operating, hardening, and continuously improving the production infrastructure that powers the Peakon Agent, multi-agent architectures, AI Features and related ML workloads. This ownership spans deployments, monitoring, and on-call workflows. Key operational functions include managing the entire deployment lifecycle for the Peakon Agent and other AI Features, ensuring the reliability of long-running agentic loops, memory stores, and tool-use environments, while contributing to driving operational excellence across the ML platform. Furthermore, the role involves building and maintaining tooling to surface agent trajectory and evaluation data, supporting performance testing, latency benchmarking, and load simulations specific to LLM-driven applications, and collaborating on the automation of essential security upgrades for ML dependencies.

About You

Basic Qualifications Summary

  • Minimum 8 years of relevant industry experience. Holds a Bachelor’s/Master or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
  • Proven track record as an MLOps or ML-savvy SRE/Platform Engineer supporting production-grade LLM and agentic systems. Proficient in Python and frameworks like LangChain and LlamaIndex.
  • Hands-on experience operating containerized services (Docker, Kubernetes) using Git-based workflows (GitOps, GitHub Actions). Solid understanding of modern ML stacks (platforms, feature stores, registries, messaging layers) or deep platform engineering background.
  • Demonstrated ability to own production infrastructure end-to-end—managing monitoring, incident response, rollbacks, and continuous reliability/uptime improvements.
  • Deep understanding of the model development lifecycle, specifically regarding model monitoring, regression tracking, and automated evaluation using tools like LangSmith.
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills under pressure, acting as a bridge between ML engineers, backend teams, and central platform/security specialists.

Other Qualifications Summary

  • Solid knowledge of data science principles and ML algorithms applied directly to LLMs, RAG, and autonomous decision-making agents.
  • Experience leading model-building processes, including advanced fine-tuning, alignment techniques, prompt engineering, and simulations of agent behaviors.
  • Strong understanding of software development principles coupled with demonstrated proficiency in System Design and Architectural Governance to deploy, scale, and maintain high-availability ML models.
  • Expertise in threat modeling and security for ML/agent systems to enforce strict behavioral guardrails. Proven experience navigating highly regulated enterprise environments to ensure data auditability, clear ownership boundaries, and strict compliance.

Title: Research Scientist
Description:
Research Scientist (Lipids)

Our story goes back more than 150 years, to the chilly southern coast of Scandinavia. From our early success in Sweden and Denmark, we’ve grown into the world’s leading specialist producer of plant-based oils, employing more than 4,000 people across the globe.

We work with our customers to make the products you love even better. The oils we produce go into making your chocolate extra-creamy or putting the crunch into your croissant. And it’s not just food — we work across industries to make products that are higher quality, healthier and better for the planet.

Everything we do is about Making Better Happen™.

AAK Locations

This position is based in one of our offices: Malmo (SE), Karlshamn (SE) or Aarhus (DK).

About The Role

Are you passionate about transforming cutting-edge science into real-world solutions? At AAK, you'll have the opportunity to explore breakthrough lipid technologies that can redefine the future of food and plant-based ingredients.

As a Research Scientist, you'll combine hands-on research with external collaboration to drive the development of next-generation lipid-based technologies. Working at the intersection of science and innovation, you'll design and lead research studies, evaluate emerging technologies, and collaborate with startups, customers, universities, and research partners to bring new ideas into AAK's innovation pipeline.

From oleogels and oleosomes to advanced structuring, delivery and encapsulation systems, you'll help unlock the potential of fats and lipids while identifying and integrating the latest scientific advancements into future products and technologies. If you're excited by curiosity-driven research, cross-functional collaboration, and making a tangible impact, this is an opportunity to help shape what comes next.

Responsibilities


Design, plan, and execute laboratory- and pilot-scale experiments to develop next-generation lipid-based technologies and applications.
Analyze and interpret experimental data, providing actionable insights, technical recommendations, and clear reports.
Develop and optimize formulations, processes, and analytical methods to support technology maturation.
Explore and evaluate emerging technologies, identifying opportunities aligned with AAK's innovation strategy.
Collaborate with R&D, Operations, Quality, Commercial teams, and external partners to advance innovation and ensure research outcomes support project objectives.
Build and maintain strong internal and external networks, including customers, startups, and academic partners.
Present scientific findings and complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, helping identify and develop business opportunities.
Maintain accurate documentation and ensure compliance with quality, safety, and regulatory standards.
Stay up to date with scientific advances and industry trends relevant to AAK's products and markets.
Contribute to intellectual property generation, including patents and technical documentation.



About You


Degree (MSc or equivalent) in a relevant scientific or engineering discipline; PhD highly desirable.
Substantial theoretical knowledge as well as proven experience in laboratory or applied research within food technology, organic chemistry, biochemistry, life sciences or other relevant fields.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Effective collaboration and communication skills, both written and verbal. Understanding of how to communicate with several stakeholders, both technical and non-technical.
Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks/projects simultaneously.
Knowledge of safety, quality, environmental, and regulatory requirements relevant to the role.
Understanding of how to navigate in a complex organization with several stakeholders.
Desire to understand and learn how business strategy interacts and give direction to innovation.
Understanding of IP and especially patents is highly desired.



Contact and information

Please apply through our career portal. For further information about the position, please contact Sr Talent Acquisition Specialist: Valerija Usova: valerija.usova@aak.com

Please note: Due to the holiday season, applications will be reviewed at the beginning of August. We appreciate your patience and look forward to reviewing your application.

About AAK

Everything AAK does is about Making Better Happen™. We specialize in plant-based oils that are the value-adding ingredients in many of the products people love to consume. We make these products better tasting, healthier, and more sustainable. At the heart of AAK’s offer is Customer Co-Development, combining our desire to understand what better means for each customer, with the unique flexibility of our production assets, and deep knowledge of many products and industries, including Chocolate and confectionery, Bakery, Dairy, Plant-based Foods, Special Nutrition, Foodservice and Personal Care. Our 4,000 employees support our close collaboration with customers through 25 regional sales offices, 15 dedicated Customer Innovation Centers, and the support of more than 20 production facilities. Listed on Nasdaq Stockholm and with our headquarters in Malmö, Sweden, AAK has been Making Better Happen™ for more than 150 years.

AAK prohibits discrimination based on race, color, sex, sexual orientation, age, national origin, physical or mental disability, religion, veteran status, and any other class of individuals protected from discrimination under state or federal law in any aspect of the access to, admission, or treatment of students in its programs and activities, or employment and application for employment.

Title: Open Talent Pool - all DHI locations
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At DHI, water, environment and sustainability meet innovation. Join us in enabling a sustainable future for water!

Looking to make an impact on our planet? At DHI, you can. We work with water in all its forms – combining deep domain knowledge, advanced digital technology, and more than 60 years of research and innovation to help society use, manage and live with water sustainably. With over 1,100 engineers and specialists across 26 countries, we are continually expanding our global community of experts.

As we grow, new opportunities emerge throughout the year. Even when a specific vacancy isn’t yet published, we welcome proactive applications from individuals who want to contribute to our mission – including senior specialist with years of experience or students, recent graduates and early career professionals who are excited to build their future in the water sector.

Why submit an open application?

Our business evolves quickly, and new roles – technical, scientific, digital and business-oriented – are created as projects grow and priorities shift. Submitting an open application adds you to our Open Talent Pool, enabling us to reach out when a suitable opportunity arises, often before it is advertised.

Before you submit your open application, please make sure to check our current vacancies as well.

Opportunities for Students & Early Career Talent

We strongly encourage students and early career professionals to apply.

At DHI, You Can


Join as an intern, student assistant, thesis collaboration partner or industry master program student
Contribute to global water projects alongside experienced specialists
Apply your academic knowledge to real-world environmental and digital challenges
Grow your skills in engineering, modelling, climate resilience, data science, software development, sustainability and more
Work in a culture that supports experimentation, learning and exploration


If you are studying or recently graduated in engineering, environmental science, climate studies, digital technology, or related fields – and want to help shape a sustainable future – your application is very welcome.

Profiles we are interested in

We invite applications from experienced professionals, graduates, and students with interest or expertise in:


Engineering & Environmental Science Coastal, marine, environmental, hydraulic, climate, civil, ecosystem modelling
Data, Digital & Technology Software development, data science, machine learning, AI, digital product development
Consulting & Project Delivery Advisory services, modelling, client engagement, project/programme management
Sustainability & ESG
Corporate Functions Legal, compliance, HR, finance, operations, commercial roles


We value cross disciplinary thinkers—if your background doesn’t fit into a category, we still encourage you to apply.

What We Offer

When you join DHI, you are empowered to be an explorer—to innovate, collaborate, and continuously learn. You’ll be part of a global organisation committed to protecting water ecosystems and developing sustainable solutions for communities, cities, industries and the planet.

Positions May Be


Fulltime or parttime
Based in various global locations or open to flexible arrangements
Project based or permanent, depending on organisational needs


How To Apply


Please submit your application through our careers page under “Open Talent”. If you are interested in other locations, please indicate that clearly.


Kindly Attach


Your CV
A brief note outlining your area of interests, skills and how you found DHI
List your preferred DHI location(s)
Links to portfolio, publications, or project work (optional)


Learn more about DHI www.dhigroup.com

Title: Ansvarlig for data science og analyse (Data scientist)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:


Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.



Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:


Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.



Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer


Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  



Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:

  • Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
  • Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
  • Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
  • Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
  • Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.


Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:

  • Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
  • Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
  • Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
  • Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
  • Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
  • Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.


Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer

  • Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
  • Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
  • Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  


Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Herlev, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About The Job

You will join a growing AI team of 11 talented colleagues with a shared mission: transforming data and AI into measurable business value for Flatpay and our merchants.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will design, build, and productionize GenAI-powered products and pipelines that support business-critical decisions across the organization. A key area of focus will be customer-facing conversational AI, where creativity, strong product thinking, and a user-centred mindset are essential for developing cutting-edge solutions.

In addition, you will work on AI-driven document, image, and audio processing systems that power critical business processes and analytics. You will collaborate closely with the broader AI team while taking strong ownership of complex projects and driving them from ideation to scalable production systems.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex, ambiguous problems with modern AI technologies and who has the technical judgment to turn ideas into robust, maintainable, and high-impact products. You will work across the entire AI product lifecycle — from data engineering and model development to deployment and user-facing applications — while helping set a high standard for applied machine learning, LLMs, and generative AI at Flatpay.

We are looking for someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment, takes end-to-end ownership, and is comfortable engaging directly with stakeholders to identify opportunities, shape solutions, align technical direction with business needs, and continuously drive improvement. As a senior member of the team, you will also be expected to contribute to technical direction, raise the quality bar, and support the growth of those around you.

What You'll Do


Design, build, and maintain production-grade GenAI applications and pipelines using Python, MLflow, Databricks, and leading AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
Develop AI-powered solutions including customer-facing conversational AI, LLM-driven data enrichment and analytics workflows, and audio, image, and multimodal processing systems for both internal and customer-facing products.
Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested production Python code, including automated tests, monitoring, guardrails, validation frameworks, and fail-safe mechanisms to ensure reliability, security, and robustness in AI systems.
Build, orchestrate, and maintain data and AI workflows in Databricks, including pipelines, jobs, workflows, model serving endpoints, and custom applications deployed on the Databricks platform.
Leverage cloud platforms such as GCP, AWS, and Azure to provision, deploy, and scale AI services, including LLMs.
Integrate, optimize, and manage AI workflows that leverage external platforms and services, including tools such as ElevenLabs, Metabase, and other third-party providers.
Collaborate closely with data engineers to ensure AI products are built on reliable, scalable, and well-governed data foundations.
Drive complex projects from proof of concept through production deployment, monitoring, ongoing optimization, and business adoption.
Make technical design decisions, evaluate trade-offs, and help define best practices for building reliable, secure, and scalable AI systems.
Mentor and support other team members through code reviews, technical sparring, and sharing practical knowledge across AI engineering, machine learning, and production GenAI systems.


Who You Are


You have 5+ years of industry experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, AI Engineering, or a similar technical role, with a strong track record of delivering production AI or ML systems.
You have hands-on experience building and maintaining customer-facing GenAI applications and pipelines in production and understand the importance of automated testing, monitoring, guardrails, and reliability in production AI systems.
You have experience using LLMs from multiple state-of-the-art providers of frontier and open-source models, and you have a deep understanding of the strengths and trade-offs of different frontier and open-source models.
You have strong experience building applications that leverage retrieval-augmented generation, tool calling, agents, or other modern LLM architectures.
You are highly comfortable developing production solutions in Python and working with libraries such as Transformers and the SDKs of leading LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and others.
You are comfortable working in Databricks or similar cloud-based data and AI platforms, including building pipelines, workflows, model serving endpoints, and production deployments.
You have strong PySpark, Pandas, and SQL skills and are comfortable working with large, structured datasets.
You are pragmatic and able to turn ambiguous business problems into concrete technical solutions, while helping stakeholders understand trade-offs and opportunities.
You are comfortable taking ownership of high-impact projects, working independently, and collaborating directly with stakeholders across the business.
You contribute positively to team standards, technical direction, and knowledge sharing, and you enjoy helping others become better at building high-quality AI systems.


Why Flatpay?


Impact: Ship AI and data solutions that create real impact in one of Europe’s fastest-growing companies.
Meaningful products: Build models that help merchants and colleagues make better decisions, act on insights, and improve workflows.
Learning and growth: Develop across forecasting, production ML, data engineering, and AI in a supportive team.
Flat structure: Flat structure and a strong action-over-process culture
Competitive salary
Vacation: 6 weeks paid
Relaxed atmosphere with optional social events such as minigolf evenings, hackathons, and company parties in exciting off-site locations.
Co-workers with a passion for data and AI, and a passion for sharing knowledge daily.
Pension and health insurance, as well as almost unlimited company merch.


About Us

Welcome to Flatpay!

Flatpay is not just another payment solution; we are dedicated to innovating the market for payment solutions and financial services in the SME segment with our customer-centric and transparent services.

Today, we are one of the fastest-growing fintech companies out of the Nordics - and Denmark's fastest unicorn!

Flatpay is more than just work. We believe in fostering a vibrant and fun work culture where our ambitious colleagues can thrive. As part of our team, you will be one of +2.000 passionate colleagues dedicated to making a difference in the world of payments.

What sets Flatpay apart is our dedication. We continuously strive to stay ahead of the curve, adapting our solutions to meet our client's evolving needs.

Let's shape the future of merchants together!

Title: Softwareingeniør
Description:
Algorithm Engineer / Quantitative Research Engineer (Power Trading)




Byg næste generation af algoritmisk power trading

Hos Ykasth Commodities bygger vi en ny type energihandelshus.

I dag består vores forretning af dygtige og erfarne power tradere, som hver dag handler de europæiske elmarkeder og træffer komplekse beslutninger baseret på markedsindsigt og erfaring.

Nu ønsker vi at tage næste skridt.




Vores ambition er at kombinere det bedste fra human trading og algorithmic trading. Vi tror på, at de stærkeste resultater opnås, når erfarne tradere arbejder tæt sammen med avancerede algoritmer, machine learning og kvantitative modeller – hvor teknologi understøtter og forstærker menneskelige beslutninger.




Derfor søger vi en Algorithm Engineer / Quantitative Research Engineer, som skal være med til at opbygge vores algoritmiske handelsplatform og udvikle de modeller, der skal drive vores fremtidige handelsstrategier.

Erfaring med power trading er ikke et krav. Vi leder efter mennesker, der kan løse komplekse problemer, tænke analytisk og bygge robuste systemer. Har du en stærk baggrund inden for datalogi, matematik, statistik, AI, machine learning eller et beslægtet teknisk område, kan vi lære dig energimarkederne.




Du er måske nyuddannet med en kandidat- eller Ph.d.-grad, eller du har allerede et par års erfaring – det vigtigste for os er ikke antallet af år på CV'et, men dit talent, din nysgerrighed og din evne til at omsætte teori til løsninger.




Er du nyuddannet, vil vi gerne have, at du vedlægger dit karakterudskrift sammen med din ansøgning og dit CV. Karakterer er ikke det eneste, vi lægger vægt på, men de giver os et godt indblik i din faglige baggrund og dine styrker.




Dine arbejdsopgaver

Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med vores tradere og udviklere om at:

Udvikle statistiske modeller og machine learning-løsninger til energimarkederne.
Bygge forecasting-modeller for priser, ubalancer og markedsadfærd.
Designe og implementere algoritmiske handelsstrategier.
Udvikle backtesting-platforme og simuleringsværktøjer.
Analysere store datamængder fra markeds-, vejr- og produktionsdata.
Optimere modeller med fokus på performance, robusthed og skalerbarhed.
Bidrage til udviklingen af vores interne trading-platform.




Vi forestiller os, at du

Har en kandidat- eller Ph.d.-grad i datalogi, matematik, statistik, machine learning, AI, fysik, økonomi eller et beslægtet teknisk studie.
Er nyuddannet eller har nogle års erfaring – begge dele er interessante for os.
Har stærke analytiske evner og interesse for algoritmer, data og modellering.
Har erfaring med Python og gerne C++, Go eller Rust.
Har arbejdet med machine learning, optimering eller kvantitativ modellering gennem studie, forskning eller erhvervserfaring.
Kan omsætte komplekse problemstillinger til praktiske løsninger.
Er nysgerrig, ambitiøs og motiveres af at skabe målbare resultater.




Erfaring fra finans- eller energisektoren er en fordel, men ikke et krav.




Teknologier

Vi arbejder blandt andet med:

Python
Go
TypeScript
SQL
Docker
Git
Cloud-infrastruktur
Machine Learning
Time Series Forecasting
Large-scale Data Processing




Det tilbyder vi

Muligheden for at være med til at bygge en algoritmisk handelsplatform fra bunden.
Et lille, ambitiøst team med stor indflydelse og korte beslutningsveje.
Tæt samarbejde med erfarne power tradere, hvor markedsindsigt og teknologi går hånd i hånd.
Spændende tekniske udfordringer med stor forretningsmæssig betydning.
Konkurrencedygtig lønpakke og attraktive ansættelsesvilkår.




Om Ykasth Commodities

Ykasth Commodities er et nyt europæisk energihandelsselskab med base i Aarhus. Vi handler på de europæiske elmarkeder og investerer målrettet i teknologi, automatisering og kvantitative modeller.

Vores ambition er at skabe en virksomhed, hvor software, data og handel går hånd i hånd – og hvor de bedste resultater opstår i samspillet mellem dygtige mennesker og avancerede algoritmer.

Hvis du brænder for algoritmer, data og komplekse optimeringsproblemer – og gerne vil være med til at bygge noget helt fra bunden – vil vi meget gerne høre fra dig.




Send venligst din ansøgning til Rasmus Eriksen på re@ykasth-commodities.com. Vi ønsker stillingen besat hurtigst muligt og afholder derfor samtaler løbende.




Har du tekniske spørgsmål vedrørende stillingen, er du velkommen til at rette henvendelse til Sebastian Godske på sg@ykasth-commodities.com.

Title: Lead Data Scientist / Analytics Engineer to TxB Analytics in Nordea
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job ID: 5146

The banking industry is undergoing a period of dynamic change and Transaction Banking (TxB) – the Payments, Cards and Trade Solution area – is at the heart of the digital, instant and open revolution. We are looking for a Lead Data Scientist / Analytics Engineer with high stakeholder management skills to create actionable customer insights, identify new revenue opportunities, improve execution on strategies utilising data and further develop or analytical platform.

About Our Team

Welcome to the TxB Analytics team; the data science team that enables TxB and our customers to become more data driven. We add value by creating actionable customer insights to optimise existing business while at the same time exploring new revenue opportunities and improving efficiency. To be successful we need a tech savvy and analytical mind with great business understanding. We aim to be courageous and explorative in our approach which means you’ll have many opportunities to learn and grow as you build your career with us. Will you help us take the next step with our use of data?

Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.

Main responsibilities in this role:


Work closely with our most important stakeholders, understand their needs, capture requirements and conduct workshops with them.
Functional leadership of the area software engineering and ML/AI to strengthen the team in the area.
Utilise data analytics to build analytics products so our stakeholders can make important strategic decisions, grow income, improve operational efficiency and develop Nordea’s product offering.
Identify business opportunities (e.g. sales leads, merchant intelligence opportunities, etc) utilising internal and external data.
Apply data science techniques such as statistical modelling, machine learning and Artificial Intelligence.
Be responsible for the development of our technical analytical platform to make it fit for purpose for Data Science.
Support and collaborate closely with other team members.


Who You Are

Your background and skills include:


You have experience working as a Data Scientist or similar where you have experiences with analytical and quantitative problem solving using advanced statistical techniques, machine learning methods and AI.
You have a business consulting mindset and are proactive working with stakeholders to understand their needs and how analytics can help them.
You have good end-to-end software engineering skills.
You have deep knowledge in SQL and programming, e.g. Python.
Solid experience in Airflow, dbt and Snowflake (or similar tools).
You are familiar with Power BI (or similar business intelligence tools).
You have data/analytics engineering experience.
You are business minded, analytical and have excellent communication skills in English (both in text and spoken).
You are a social, curious and a pragmatic team player.
Experience in Requirements Management and workshop facilitation would an advantage.
Experience in Transaction Banking or Payments/Cards would be an advantage.
You have a MSc/BSc/BA degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, physics or related quantitative degree.


What We Offer

People come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.

A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.

Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.

Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.

If this sounds like you, get in touch!

Next steps

Submit your application no later than 07/08/2026. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Mikael Simonsson at mikael.simonsson@nordea.com.

We lead change. We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.

Title: AI/Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world. Capgemini’s Global Insights & Data business line is a market leader in data, analytics, and cloud platforms across multiple industries, including financial services, life sciences, consumer products, utilities, and the public sector.

Your Role

As an AI/Data Engineer, you will design, build, and operate modern data platforms and data products that enable analytics and AI-driven use cases. Working closely with clients and cross-functional teams, you will deliver scalable, reliable, and compliant data solutions in complex enterprise and regulated environments. You will contribute to developing robust data pipelines and ensuring that data products are production-ready, governed, and aligned with enterprise standards.

In this role you will play a key role in:


Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and data products that support analytics and AI use cases. You will develop and maintain batch and real-time processing solutions, ensuring high data quality, reliability, and performance across ingestion and transformation layers
Collaborate with data product owners, AI/ML specialists, and platform teams to support feature engineering, model training, and operationalization of AI solutions, including preparing and transforming data for AI models and ensuring traceability and reproducibility
Implement data transformations using Python and SQL, apply best practices for incremental and idempotent processing, while ensuring pipelines are observable, testable, and resilient
Contribute to enterprise standards for data governance, lineage, and compliance while continuously optimizing pipelines and platform performance in collaboration with cross-functional teams


Your profile


Solid experience (+6 years) as an AI/Data Engineer in enterprise environments, delivering scalable data solutions that support analytics and AI use cases
Hands-on expertise in building and operating batch and near real-time data pipelines, ensuring reliability, performance, and traceability across the data lifecycle
Experience with modern data platforms and lakehouse architecture, including tools such as Databricks and Snowflake, and working with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud Platform)
Proficiency in Python and SQL, combined with strong knowledge of modern data engineering best practices such as incremental processing, data quality validation, observability, metadata management, and lineage
Experience with data modeling (e.g., Kimball, Data Vault), distributed data formats (e.g., Parquet, Iceberg), and streaming/event-driven architectures, along with strong collaboration and communication skills in governed enterprise settings


About

What we believe you will enjoy about working here

Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you can shape your career, work with a diverse collective of experts, and help leading organizations unlock the value of data and AI responsibly.

At Capgemini, You Will


Work hands-on with modern cloud, lakehouse, and AI-enabled data platforms
Build data products and pipelines that power analytics and AI use cases
Gain exposure to large-scale, regulated, and mission-critical environments
Be a part of a collaborative culture that values innovation, entrepreneurship, and continuous learning


Application

If you are ready to take the next step in your career, designing, building, and operating modern data platforms and data products, we look forward to receiving your application.

We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as require

Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion.

Make it real | www.capgemini.com
