Title: People & Culture Generalist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
In our People & Culture team, we are committed to support all our Hobsters (i.e. colleagues) by creating a culture where people enjoy coming to work - and we need your help with continuously improving our people agenda!

Imagine being the person who keeps the whole employee journey running smoothly - from the moment someone signs their contract to the day they walk in the door, a promotion, parental leave and everything else that might happen during employee lifecycle. This is your chance to own the operational backbone of People & Culture at Hobbii, while also being the sparring partner leaders turn to when things get complicated 🤓

You will work shoulder to shoulder with our Head of People & Culture, splitting your time between administrative execution and coaching of leaders. In Hobbii we reach over 5 million passionate makers of knitting- and crochet projects across 13 countries every single week. Behind that there is a team of talented, dedicated Hobsters who need a P&C partner who's as comfortable with our internal systems and processes, as they are in a conversation about performance.

If you love the idea of owning master data and processes, but you also get energy from advising Hobsters and leaders on people matters - this role is built for you 💫

What you will actually spend your time doing


Employee lifecycle admin: Coordinating processes like onboarding, offboarding and contract changes end to end, so every Hobster's experience feels frictionless 🚀
Advisory & coaching: Being the trusted sparring partner for leaders on the legal matters (hereunder Danish employment law and collective agreements), internal policies and procedures, team dynamics, and difficult conversations.
Talent acquisition: Working with leaders to define talent needs and support recruitment process, hereunder run EASI personality tests and feedback sessions.
Performance & compensation review cycles: Coordinating annual and ongoing processes such as appraisal talks and salary reviews.
Turning data into action and visible results: From onboarding reviews, through engagement surveys and to exit interviews, you'll turn data into action and help reach the eNPS targets across teams 🎯


Who we imagine you are

About

You are detail-oriented, but never lose sight of the bigger organisational picture, and you're just as comfortable saying "I'll check the legal aspects" as "Let's talk about how that conversation went." We will be thrilled if you tick these boxes:


3-5 years of experience as an HR Generalist, with demonstrated hands-on experience in HR administration, recruitment and employee relations cases.
Thorough knowledge of Danish employment law, including Funktionærloven, and local labour relations.
Solid consulting skills - you can build strong relations based on trust and support leaders even through a sensitive situation.
Comfortable operating independently on day-to-day tasks while collaborating closely with the Head of P&C on the more complex projects.
A can-do attitude and energised by juggling different responsibilities.
Professional fluency in English and Danish.
Knitter and/or crocheter is an additional talent we appreciate 🧶


The culture and the team

Working at Hobbii should feel exciting, fun and meaningful. Our Hobbii norms guide how we work together: for example we help one another succeed, and learn and grow together. We are a bunch of ca. 200 Hobsters spread across our vibrant HQ in Copenhagen NV, cool Warehouse in Greve, 5 amazing stores located in Denmark and 1 store in Germany.

You will be based at HQ where we each day enjoy lunch from Meyers, barista-quality coffee and great energy from our dear colleagues 🤗 Some of the employee benefits that we offer are 6 weeks of holiday, employer-paid pension and private health insurance.

This role reports to the Head of People & Culture, and as their backup, you'll get direct exposure to strategic decisions and a real say when it matters most.

We're excited to welcome someone into this role as soon as possible, which is why we'll be inviting candidates to interviews on an ongoing basis 📆

Ready to apply?

We have dropped the traditional cover letter. Just upload your CV, link to your LinkedIn profile if you have one, and answer a few questions in our application form. It only takes a couple of minutes. We cannot wait to hear from you! 🤩

Title: Student Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Skape Bio we're looking for a motivated Student Assistant to support lab operations and help keep the lab organised.

Skape Bio is an early-stage biotech company developing de novo designed miniprotein therapeutics for GPCRs, combining computational design, protein engineering, screening technologies, and translational science.

We are a collaborative, hands-on team based at the BioInnovation Institute in central Copenhagen, an innovation hub with exposure to the biotech startup ecosystem.

Responsibilities


Preparing culture media, agar plates, buffers, and reagents
Restocking lab supplies across three floors
Keeping track of stocks and placing orders for consumables
Picking up packages and organising inventory
Autoclaving glassware and lab materials
Supporting lab clean-up and waste disposal routines
Chemicals registration and laboratory risk assessments


About You


Enrolled in a relevant life science education: Biochemistry, Molecular Biomedicine, Biotechnology, Biology, or similar
Preferably late bachelor’s or early master’s student, with at least one year left of studies
Previous lab experience required; experience with buffer preparation important; DNA prep and/or cell culture is a plus
Organised, responsible, and reliable with attention to detail
Open, communicative, and forthcoming


Working Hours And Compensation


Expected workload is 12–15 hours per week, with availability for at least one full working day per week of minimum 6 hours.
Compensation is 175 DKK per hour (plus 10% pension).


Apply for the job

Do you want to join our team as our new Student Assistant? Then we'd love to hear about you!

Deadline August 28th 2026.

Title: Student Data Scientist – Conversational AI
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you a curious and motivated student looking to get experience with the latest advances in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI (GenAI)? We’re looking for a Student Assistant to support our AI team on real-world projects that improve language technology

This is your chance to gain valuable experience, contribute to impactful solutions, and grow your skills in an inspiring and supportive environment.

Who We Are

The Conversational AI team is diverse team Data Scientists and AI Engineers with various educational and cultural backgrounds. We are responsible for applying AI in Danske Banks customer facing solutions ranging anywhere from ML models used for personalisation and marketing optimisation to chatbots and advisor assistants.

We pride ourselves on being at the forefront of the latest technology and research identifying use case where AI can be applied to create value for customer and the bank.

🧠 What You’ll Work On

As a student assistant, you’ll contribute to the development and testing of GenAI applications, such as chatbots, knowledge assistants, and NLP tools. You’ll work closely with our experienced data scientists and engineers on:


Preparing and cleaning datasets for NLP tasks
Evaluating the output of LLMs and fine-tuning prompts
Assisting with prototype development using tools like OpenAI, Hugging Face, and LangChain
Supporting testing and benchmarking of RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) systems
Documenting findings, results, and code for internal use


📚What We’re Looking For


You’re currently studying a relevant degree (Data Science, Computer Science, AI, or related)
You have a strong interest in NLP and GenAI
You’re comfortable coding in Python and familiar with basic AI libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, pandas)
You’re structured, proactive, and motivated to learn
You can work 15–20 hours per week alongside your studies


💡 Nice to Have (But Not Required)


Familiarity with LLMs (e.g., GPT models), vector databases, or chatbot development
Experience with Git, Jupyter Notebooks, or cloud platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS)


🌟 What We Offer


Hands-on experience with cutting-edge AI tools and techniques
Flexible working hours tailored to your study schedule
A collaborative and friendly team invested in your growth
A chance to make real contributions to projects used across the organization
Working in our new HQ in Copenhagen with great facilities


📩 Interested?

We would love to hear from you.

Apply as soon as possible, as we review applications on an ongoing basis and will begin interviewing candidates shortly.

Join us and help shape the future of Conversational AI at Danske Bank.

About Us

Danske Bank is a Nordic bank with bridges to the world around us. For 150 years, we have supported people and businesses in releasing their potential. A career with us is an opportunity to join a community of 22,000 colleagues in a culture where we are committed to Teaming Up, Owning It and Being Open. Together, we are on a journey to transform Danske Bank into a better bank. For our customers, our employees and the societies around us.

Title: DATA SCIENCE CONSULTANT COPENHAGEN
Description:
We are currently recruiting for our Summer 2026 intake, with an expected start date in September 2026.




Management Solutions is an international consulting firm whose core mission is to deliver business, risk, financial, organisational and process-related advisory services, targeting both functional aspects and the implementation of related technologies. We currently have a multidisciplinary team (functional, mathematical, technical and systems integration) of 4,000 professionals.

We operate through 52 offices (24 in Europe, 23 in the Americas, 3 in Asia, 1 in Africa and 1 in Oceania) from where we regularly serve clients that operate in more than 50 countries across five major geographical areas (Europe, Americas, Asia, Africa and Oceania).




Role:




Statistical data processing (Data Mining)
Artificial Intelligence (AI), including generative AI
Predictive modeling using Machine Learning and Data Science techniques
Time-series modeling (ARIMA and panel data models) and simulation models (Monte Carlo)
Development, validation and audit of financial, risk, marketing and business-oriented models
R&D projects




Requirements:




Recent graduates or final year students.
Should desirably have knowledge of Artificial Intelligence modeling techniques (logit, GLM, time series, random forests, clustering, XGBoost), statistical programming languages (SAS, R, Python, Matlab), big data tools and platforms (Hadoop, Hive, etc.) and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Solid academic record.
Strong computer skills.
Postgraduate studies and/or specialized courses are an asset, especially in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Finance or similar.
Knowledge of other languages is desirable.
Get-up-and-go attitude, maturity, responsibility and strong work ethic.
Strong ability to learn quickly.
Able to integrate easily into multidisciplinary teams.




What we offer:




We offer you the possibility to join a firm that provides all you need to develop your talent to the fullest:




Working in the highest-profile consulting projects in the industry,
for the largest companies, leaders of their respective markets,
alongside top industry management as they face challenges at the national and global level,
as part of an extraordinary team of professionals whose values and corporate culture are a benchmark for the industry




Training:

Ongoing training plan, with approximately 10% of business turnover spent on training.
Specialist knowledge courses, external expert courses, professional skills courses and language courses.
Last year our staff as a whole received over 400,000 hours of training spanning more than 160 courses.




Career plan:

Clearly defined career plan.
Internal promotion based solely on merit.
Partnership-based management model offers all professionals the opportunity to become part of the Firm’s group of partners




Complementary experiences:




University: we maintain a close relationship with the world’s most prestigious universities.
Social Action: we organize more than 30 community support activities.
Sports Club: internal and external tournaments.




How to apply:




If you are interested in our offer, please complete our application form online at https://www.managementsolutions.com/en/professional-careers-send-us-your-cv

Title: Manager
Description:
identity manager.




Er du klar til at tage det fulde strategiske og driftsmæssige ejerskab over et IAM-landskab i markant udvikling?

Salling Group Tech søger en ambitiøs Identity Manager til Core Tech. Her bliver du drivkraften bag en solid transformation, hvor du styrker deres governance og sikrer fuld NIS2-compliance på tværs af hele koncernen.




om rollen

Som Identity Manager får du det formelle ledelsesansvar for et team af dygtige specialister. Du bliver den bærende drivkraft i at sikre et samlet team, hvor governance, fælles standarder og høj kvalitet efterleves i hverdagen. I rollen vil du balancere direkte mellem langsigtede, strategiske forandringer og en sikker, stabil daglig drift. Du får en central placering i Salling Group Tech, hvor du fungerer som brobygger og formidler, der sikrer opbakning og tæt samarbejde på tværs af Salling Group Tech.




Primære opgaver og ansvarsområder:




NIS2 Compliance & PIM/JIT: Sikre fuld compliance (Artikel 21) via sporbare audit trails samt rulle PIM og JIT ud på alle kritiske platforme.
Skabe ensartethed på tværs af spredte platforme, herunder Azure AD, SAP, cloud og specifikt GitHub.
Stabilisere JML-processer, eksekvere adgang reviews og opbygge en fast model for Service Account Governance.
Fastsætte rammer for MFA og certifikater samt lukke logningshuller via effektiv SIEM-integration.
Oversætte tekniske sikkerhedskrav til forretningsværdi og drive en kultur, hvor standardisering ses som en styrke.
Drive og motivere teamet.




hvem er du?

Du brænder for at skabe struktur, kvalitet og gennemsigtighed i et komplekst it-miljø. Som person er du en tydelig kommunikatør og en stærk relation bygger på tværs af tekniske specialister og forretningens ledere. Som leder optræder du engageret, nysgerrigt og motiveres af at drive specialister. Du arbejder strategisk, men bevarer en udpræget pragmatisk og driftsorienteret tilgang, så løsningerne altid fungerer i praksis. Derudover besidder du et naturligt ledertalent og holder dig proaktivt opdateret på de nyeste trends indenfor dit felt.




Vi ser gerne, at du:




Har en solid og dokumenteret erfaring inden for Identity & Access Management (IAM) fra komplekse it-landskaber, hvor cloud, SaaS og on-premise-miljøer smelter sammen.
Har erfaring med at drive større forandringer, indføre fælles standarder og operationalisere stærk it-governance.
Enten er etableret leder eller en tung senior profil (f.eks. med erfaring som projektleder eller teamkoordinator inden for IAM), der har drevet store initiativer og er helt klar til formelt personaleansvar.
Har praktisk kendskab til eller erfaring med implementering og modning af teknologier som SSO, NERM, PIM, JIT, MFA og bruger certifikater.
Forstår at omsætte regulatoriske compliance-krav (særligt NIS2 Artikel 21) til konkrete processer, herunder opbygning af audit trails, logning og SIEM-integration.
Taler og skriver flydende både dansk og engelsk på forretningsniveau.




hvorfor Salling Group?

Du får en central position i hjertet af Danmarks største detailkoncern, hvor high performance er et gennemgående tema.




Du får et stort ansvar med masser af udfordringer i en flad struktur, hvor der er kort fra tanke til handling.
De er en stor familie, der går efter målet i fællesskab, og arbejder ud fra værdierne Integrity, Passionate, Agile, Competitive og Efficient (IPACE).




ansøgning og kontakt

Randstad varetager rekrutteringsprocessen på vegne af Salling Group. Hvis stillingen har din interesse, bedes du ansøge via dette site.




For mere detaljeret information om stillingen, bedes du kontakte chefkonsulent og functional expert, Kasper Kjærgaard på +45 61 95 92 51. Alle henvendelser vil blive behandlet fortroligt.




om randstad

Randstad er verdens største HR-udbyder og ‘partner for talent’. Vi giver lige muligheder til alle uanset baggrund og hjælper kandidater med at forblive relevante på et hastigt skiftende arbejdsmarked. Vi tror på et inkluderende arbejdsmarked, hvor alle kan være sig selv og føle sig velkomne.




Vores markedsindsigt og store kendskab til arbejdsmarkedet hjælper virksomheder med at opbygge en kvalificeret, mangfoldig og fleksibel arbejdsstyrke, og vi støtter mennesker og virksomheder i at nå deres fulde potentiale. Det er det, vi er her for, og det er det, der motiverer os hver dag.




Randstad DK er en del af den internationale Randstad Group. Vi formidler dagligt arbejde til over 600.000 mennesker verden over og er førende inden for rekruttering, karriererådgivning og fleksibel arbejdskraft i Danmark, med afdelinger i København, Aarhus, Aalborg og Kolding

Title: Reprint Technician
Description:
Join our Hinnerup team as a new Reprint Technician

Help give technology a second life. Join Foxway and play an important role in extending the lifecycle of IT equipment while contributing to a more sustainable future.

About the Role:

As a Reprint Technician, you will be responsible for restoring laptop keyboards through our reprinting process, ensuring every device meets our quality standards before it reaches its next user.

No previous experience with our systems is required—we provide full training. What matters most is your attention to detail, interest in technology, and willingness to learn.

What you will do:


Reprint laptop keyboards using specialised machinery
Operate and monitor production equipment
Perform quality checks to ensure high standards
Follow production schedules and work instructions
Support other production tasks when needed


We believe you have


A keen eye for detail and quality
An interest in technology and practical hands-on work
Confidence using tools and technical equipment
Ability to follow processes and work instructions accurately
Good English and Danish communication skills
A proactive attitude and willingness to learn
Ability to work independently as well as part of a team


No previous experience with our systems is required—we will provide full training.

Work Conditions


Full-time on-site at Hinnerup, Denmark


Why Join Foxway?


Be part of something meaningful – Help reduce electronic waste by giving IT equipment a second life.
Learn and grow – Receive comprehensive onboarding and training while developing new technical skills.
Supportive team – Join experienced colleagues who enjoy working together and helping each other succeed.
International workplace – Collaborate in a growing company with colleagues across Europe.
Career opportunities – As Foxway grows, so do the opportunities to develop your career.


Help us make a difference!

We are reviewing applications on an ongoing basis, so if this sounds like the next step in your career, apply quickly!

How does Foxway operate?

Foxway is a fast-growing and dynamic organization with an international team of nearly 1,300 people. We operate across multiple countries, with key hubs across Europe.

Within Foxway, Recommerce Computing & Enterprise (C&E) focuses on giving IT equipment a second life through refurbishment, reuse, and resale helping businesses maximize value while reducing environmental impact.

Our mission is to make sustainable technology accessible to everyone by extending the lifecycle of computers and IT equipment and contributing to a more circular future.

By joining us, you become part of a purpose-driven, international team that is shaping a more sustainable tech industry.

Foxway is a fast-growing and dynamic organization with an international team of nearly 1,300 people. We operate across multiple countries, with key hubs across Europe.

Within Foxway, Recommerce Computing & Enterprise (C&E) focuses on giving IT equipment a second life through refurbishment, reuse, and resale helping businesses maximize value while reducing environmental impact.

Our mission is to make sustainable technology accessible to everyone by extending the lifecycle of computers and IT equipment and contributing to a more circular future.

By joining us, you become part of a purpose-driven, international team that is shaping a more sustainable tech industry.

Title: Associate Specialist, Workplace Experience
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Role Purpose

The Associate Specialist, Workplace Experience is responsible for delivering a safe, efficient, and consistently high-quality workplace experience across Pandora's Global Office locations in Copenhagen.

The role ensures that workplace services, facilities operations, health and safety standards, and employee-facing environments operate seamlessly, enabling employees to do their best work. Through strong operational coordination, supplier management, and continuous improvement, the role contributes to a workplace that is welcoming, compliant, well-maintained, and aligned with Pandora's culture and service standards.

Key Responsibilities

Workplace Experience & Operations


Ensure a consistently high standard across office spaces, meeting rooms, kitchens, collaboration areas, and employee amenities.
Deliver a seamless workplace experience by proactively resolving operational issues and responding to employee needs.
Conduct regular workplace inspections and drive follow-up actions to maintain quality, functionality, and presentation standards.
Support a professional and welcoming experience for employees, visitors, and guests.


Facilities & Supplier Management


Ensure workplace services are delivered effectively through strong coordination of suppliers and service partners.
Monitor supplier performance, drive service improvements, and resolve operational issues in a timely manner.
Support effective management of workplace budgets, purchase orders, invoices, and operational spend.
Maintain accurate workplace inventories and ensure facilities resources are available and fit for purpose.


Health, Safety & Business Continuity


Contribute to maintaining a safe and compliant workplace in accordance with Danish legislation and company standards.
Coordinate workplace inspections, corrective actions, and safety initiatives to reduce workplace risk.
Maintain clear workplace procedures, records, and documentation to support operational consistency and compliance.
Support emergency preparedness, business continuity, and wellbeing initiatives across the Global Office.


Workspace Planning & Projects


Deliver office moves, seating changes, and workplace projects with minimal disruption to business operations.
Ensure workspaces are fully operational and ready for employees from day one.
Support continuous improvement initiatives that enhance workplace efficiency, utilisation, and employee experience.


Employee Experience & Engagement


Support the successful delivery of office events, workplace initiatives, and employee engagement activities.
Build strong relationships with stakeholders across the business to understand needs and identify opportunities for improvement.
Continuously seek ways to enhance workplace services, increase efficiency, and improve employee satisfaction.


Skills & Experience


Experience in workplace, facilities, office, hospitality, or community management.
Strong organisational and stakeholder management skills.
Service-oriented with a proactive and solution-focused mindset.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to operational excellence.
Experience coordinating suppliers and managing multiple priorities simultaneously.
Comfortable working with workplace systems, technology, and AV equipment.
Financial awareness and experience supporting budget management.
Fluent in English and Danish, written and spoken.


Did we get your attention?

If you see yourself in the position and would like to become part of Pandora’s future, please do not hesitate to apply. We look forward to hearing from you.

We process applications on a continuous basis, which is why we encourage you to send your application as soon as possible. You can also read more about Pandora on our corporate site.

Icing on the cake

You will be working out of our beautiful Head Office in Copenhagen V, established in 2016 and designed to unite and inspire our teams. Besides our beautiful interior design, we are also proud of our LEED Gold Certification for Energy and Environmental Design. This means that we use as few natural resources as possible and that we use them with respect. The result is a professional and dynamic space that is unique, comfortable and inspirational – and delivers a true Pandora experience to anyone who works here or comes to visit.

About Pandora

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Pandora jewellery is sold in more than 100 countries through 7,000 points of sale, including more than 2,800 concept stores.

Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs around 39,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery with 100% recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 3

Title: Costing Engineer
Place: Smørumnedre, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about turning complex data into better business decisions?

Frontmatec is looking for a Costing Engineer to play a key role in strengthening our costing and pricing capabilities across Meat Solutions Europe.

This position is critical to ensuring accurate cost calculations, competitive pricing, and profitable project execution. You will work closely with Finance, Operations, Engineering, Sales, and Project Management, helping the business understand cost drivers and improve commercial decision-making.

What you'll do


Develop and maintain costing models for projects, equipment, and solutions.
Analyze material, labor, and manufacturing costs.
Support quotation and sales activities with accurate costing data.
Identify cost-saving opportunities and cost optimization initiatives.
Drive transparency around cost structures and profitability.
Collaborate with stakeholders across multiple business functions.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of costing processes and tools.



What we're looking for


3+ years of experience within costing, engineering, manufacturing, project business, finance, or a similar field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience working with ERP systems and advanced Excel.
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and commercial stakeholders.
Fluent in Danish and English.



This role offers a hybrid working environment. While many of your closest colleagues and stakeholders are based in Southern Denmark, you will collaborate with teams across our European organization.

Why join Frontmatec?

This is an opportunity to join a growing international organization where your work will have a direct impact on profitability, pricing strategies, and business performance. You'll become part of a collaborative environment and work alongside experienced colleagues across Europe.

If you enjoy combining technical understanding, business insight, and data-driven decision-making, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply today and help us build the foundation for smarter costing and stronger commercial performance.
Show more

Title: Centre of Excellence Lead — Data Science & Machine Learning
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Too Good To Go, we have an ambitious goal: to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together.

40% of all food produced in the world is wasted. And that has a huge impact on the health of our planet, with 10% of greenhouse gas emissions coming from food waste.

We're more than an app: we are a certified B Corporation with a mission to empower everyone to take action against food waste, so alongside our marketplace app, we create educational tools, explore new business solutions — such as our Retail Technologies offering — and influence legislation to help reduce food waste.

We're growing fast: Our community of 120 million registered users and 180,000 active partners across 20 countries have together already prevented 500+ million meals from going to waste

Your mission

We are looking for a Centre of Excellence Lead to build and grow the data science and machine learning capability at the heart of our Data and Analytics organisation.

The Centre of Excellence (CoE) brings together our Data Scientists and ML Engineers into a single, centralised team that partners with Product and business domains to solve our most important problems. As its Lead, you will own the team's technical direction, raise the bar on delivery and standards, and develop the people in it while staying close enough to the work to coach hands-on.

As a marketplace, many of our most critical challenges revolve around supply-demand dynamics, user behaviour, and marketplace efficiency. Your team works on problems such as personalisation, pricing, optimisation, and forecasting, helping us move from reactive analysis to proactive, model-driven systems.

Your role


Lead and grow the CoE team of Data Scientists and ML Engineers — owning performance management, coaching, hiring, and the development of each individual
Set and raise technical standards across the team, from problem framing and methodology through to experimentation, productionisation, and impact measurement
Stay hands-on as a player-coach — close enough to the technical work to guide design decisions, review approaches, and help unblock the team
Strengthen the DS↔ML handoff, ensuring models move from prototype to production reliably and that the two disciplines collaborate closely from the start of each initiative
Partner with Product and business leaders to identify high-impact opportunities where data science and ML can drive step-change improvements, and translate them into a focused team roadmap
Represent the CoE to Product, Engineering, and leadership stakeholders, making the case for where and how advanced methods create the most value
Partner with the People team to define the capability framework for the data science and ML domain
Champion the responsible use of AI within the team's ways of working, advocating for best practices and integrating new tools where they add genuine value


Requirements


Solid experience leading technical teams of a similar size (around 5 people), including performance management and developing engineers or scientists
A strong technical foundation in data science or machine learning — you have done the technical work yourself and can credibly coach it
Comfortable across both disciplines: you don't need to have managed both Data Scientists and ML Engineers, but you should have enough fluency in each to set direction and earn the team's trust
Track record of delivering ML or DS work that reached production and created measurable business or product impact
Strong grounding in experimentation (A/B testing), measurement, and impact evaluation
Experience with applied problems such as personalisation/recommendation, optimisation, pricing, or forecasting
A pragmatic, current view on AI its maturity, where it adds value, and how to integrate it responsibly into a team's workflow
Strong stakeholder and communication skills — able to influence Product, Engineering, and business leaders
Comfort operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a high degree of ownership
Fluent in English, with excellent written and verbal communication skills


Our values


We win together: Food waste is a big beast to fight. We believe in a #oneteam.
We raise the bar: We always push for more. We work smart, smash barriers and elevate one another.
We keep it simple: Our ambitions are bold but our solutions are simple.
We build a legacy: We're proud of the change we're driving.
We care: We always look out for each other. Caring is also about the way we do business. We do the right thing.


What we have to offer


An opportunity to work in a global social-impact company and certified B Corporation, where you can see a real and tangible impact in your role.
The chance to build and lead a centralised data science and ML capability from an early stage, shaping how advanced methods drive decisions across the company.
Work-life balance is important to us! Focus on the job to be done, not the hours spent — there is no need for overtime.
We trust our employees and encourage an autonomous environment with several opportunities to contribute, develop, and take ownership.
To be part of an international company, with over 1,200+ colleagues across 20 countries on the same important mission.
An informal environment, working alongside 170+ passionate people within Tech & Product.
We celebrate our differences and our successes and have a strong values-driven team culture.
We work in a hybrid set-up (4 days per week in our Copenhagen office) and value in-office work weekly.
We may be able to support you with a working visa in Copenhagen, please be prepared to discuss this topic further at your first-stage interview.


How to apply


We take recruitment very seriously, so please carefully read everything we have written above. Please also check our website and international media to get a good overview of Too Good To Go.
Submit your CV in English.
Please note that we only accept applications coming through our platform. No CV will be accepted by email or LinkedIn direct messaging.


A Movement for Everyone

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.

Title: Development Scientist
Place: Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Development Scientist – Organic Chemistry

Global Manufacturing & Supply

Bagsværd, Denmark

18-month temporary position

Are you passionate about applying your expertise in synthetic organic chemistry to support the life cycle management of pharmaceutical products that reach millions of patients? Do you thrive in cross-functional collaboration, process optimisation and high-quality technical documentation – without the need for practical laboratory work? If so, join us as our new Development Scientist in Bagsværd on an 18-month temporary contract.

Your new role

You will play a key role in the manufacturing of chemical entities and the chemical modification of recombinant peptides and proteins. Supporting life cycle management activities at Contract Manufacturing Organisations (CMOs), you will help secure the continuous market supply of our products and contribute scientific expertise across a wide range of projects.

Your Day-to-day Tasks Will Include


Supporting process transfers, validations and optimisation projects at CMOs
Engaging with cross-organisational groups to align on scientific and technical priorities
Generating and updating technical documentation at the right scientific level
Providing scientific input for change requests and deviations
Contributing to authorship and review of regulatory documents
Driving process optimisation initiatives in close collaboration with internal and external partners


Your new department

In Global Manufacturing & Supply (GMS), we cover the full value chain from drug and device development to marketed Novo Nordisk products. We are more than 30,000 colleagues across the globe with the shared responsibility of scaling molecules, processes, devices, and products, dedicated to supplying life-saving medicines to the patients who need them most.

Within GMS, we manufacture the pharmaceutical backbone of our many quality APIs. Our department is called Process Support and is anchored in MSAT. It consists of several teams responsible for the timely support of the processes that create our APIs. You will join a dynamic, well-functioning and cross-functional team of 13 scientists and 1 laboratory technician, who thrive on professional challenges and work with great individual responsibility and commitment. We collaborate with production, analytical departments, CMC, Regulatory Affairs, QA – and externally with CMOs Our officelocation is Bagsværd.

Your Skills & Qualifications

We are looking for a highly adaptable professional with analytical, innovative and collaborative skills, who excels both independently and within diverse teams. You bring sound judgement, decision-making ability and a drive to deliver practical solutions across multiple disciplines and external partnerships.

Essential Qualifications


M.Sc. or PhD in synthetic organic chemistry
Experience with organic synthesis; knowledge of chemical modification of peptides is an advantage
Fluency in spoken and written English


It Is Advantageous – But Not Mandatory – To Have


Experience in process chemistry, including scale-up and transfer of processes from pilot to facilities and between different sites
A background in process characterisation and validation within the pharmaceutical industry
Experience with cGMP and handling of deviations and change requests in pharmaceutical settings


Applicants without this additional experience are still strongly encouraged to apply.

Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

At Novo Nordisk, you'll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits designed with your career and life stage in mind.


Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 570,200 to 838,100 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position. The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate's skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.
Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.


Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

More information

For further information, please contact Hiring Manager Casper Mørck at +45 3079 8657.

Deadline

21 July 2026. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible.

You do not need to attach a cover letter to your application, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV. To ensure an efficient and fair process, please refrain from adding a photo in your CV. We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all job applicants.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

  • Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 570,200 to 838,100 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position. The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate's skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.
  • Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

More information

For further information, please contact Hiring Manager Casper Mørck at +45 3079 8657.

Deadline

21 July 2026. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible.

You do not need to attach a cover letter to your application, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV. To ensure an efficient and fair process, please refrain from adding a photo in your CV. We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all job applicants.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Title: Ansvarlig for data science og analyse (Data scientist)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:


Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.



Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:


Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.



Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer


Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  



Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Title: Operatør - Fuldtid - Køge
Description:
Introduktion

Vil du være med til at sikre, at de rette varer bliver leveret til vores butikker? Så er du måske vores nye operatør på det automatiserede anlæg på lageret!

På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold, og vi har et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, når det kommer til dagligvareindkøb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen, med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig. Derfor investerer vi i dig som medarbejder. Og derfor er der ingen grænser for, hvor langt du kan nå.

Som operatør Lidl får du ansvarsfulde opgaver, hvor du er med til at sikre de rette varer til vores butikker ved at understøtte driften af vores automatiserede anlæg i Køge. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, ligesom du får adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både fagligt og personligt.

Om jobbet


Være medansvarlig for den daglige drift af vores automatiserede anlæg i Køge
Overvågning, betjening og opfølgning på anlæggets driftmæssigestatus
Fejlsøgning og vedligeholdelsesopgaver under den daglige drift
Deltage i planlægning og udførelse af aktiviteter på anlægget, herunder planlagt vedligehold og tekniske forbedringer
Samarbejde med andre afdelinger om optimering af anlægget


Om dig


Du har erfaring med betjening og overvågning af produktionsanlæg
Du har erfaring med løbende proceskontrol og kvalitetsopfølgning
Din erfaring og interesse med teknik og mekanik bruger du til at sikre høj kvalitet i din opgaveløsning
Du er indstillet på at indgå i 3 holdsskift i tidsrummet 06:00 til 05:00
Du ser samarbejde som en styrke og er god til det
Erfaring med at arbejde i et internationalt miljø hvor kommunikationen er på engelsk


Vi tilbyder

Som operatør på lageret i Lidl får du:


Verdensklasse sammenhold
Ansvarsfulde opgaver
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Garanteret udvikling og uddannelse
Gode karrieremuligheder, hvis du har ambitioner om mere
Mulighed for sundhedsordning
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.


Vil du med på #teamlidl?

Sådan søger du stillingen:

Klik på ”Søg nu”

Udfyld formularen

Vedhæft dit motiverede CV

Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på tlf. 7635 0635 på hverdage indenfor normal arbejdstid eller på jobilidloest@lidl.dk.

Title: Data & Customer Retention Student Assistant (Marketing)
Place: Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you spot the one number in Excel that doesn't add up, and then get ideas for how to fix the process behind it? Then keep reading.

European Lifecare Group runs private clinics, corporate vaccination programmes, and government contracts in Denmark, the Netherlands, the UK, and Sweden. In Group Marketing, we work data-driven with customer acquisition, retention, and customer experience (CX), and we are looking for a structured student assistant to help improve our customer retention flows and keep our numbers sharp.

You will work directly with our Group Customer Experience & Lifecycle Manager while we build out our automation and data framework. Until it's fully in place, much of the work is hands-on: pulling exports, cleaning data in Excel, and importing it where it needs to go. But that is also where the opportunity is; you'll be part of the journey from manual to automated, being close enough to spot what to automate next, and gradually shift your time from maintaining the numbers to working with them.

This is a role for someone who enjoys structure, accuracy and practical problem-solving. It is not a job where you only "help with a bit of marketing". You will work with real customer data, real management reporting and real retention activities across four countries.

What You Will Learn And Be Trusted With


Help set up and maintain our retention emails: building emails in our marketing automation platform, cleaning and structuring data in Excel, and testing that everything works before send. It's hands-on, precise work, and it's what keeps everything else running
Maintain, update and participate in improving KPI sheets covering retention and customer experience across our four markets. These are shared with management teams, so accuracy and clarity matter
Prepare customer data for Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) and retention analysis (cleaning, matching, and structuring exports from our medical journal systems)
Be responsible for our A/B testing library: logging tests across email, web, and campaigns, documenting results, and keeping learnings organised
Why this job is worth your two daysReal responsibility from day one: your work will be used in reporting and decision-making across four countries
You will learn how customer data is turned into retention actions, customer experience improvements and commercial insights
You will be part of building an automation and data framework in a growing healthcare group and you will help shape our future
A workplace where we encourage smarter ways of working, and responsible use of AI can be part of that
You will get hands-on experience with tools and methods within the full retention toolkit: CRM, marketing automation, CLV analysis, and A/B testing
Flexibility that respects your studies


Who You Are


Studying business, economics, marketing, or something data/analytics-oriented
Strong in Excel, or genuinely eager to learn it (XLOOKUP and pivot tables are your daily tools here)
Precise, structured and self-driven: you double-check before you send, and you can own a recurring task and deliver on deadline
You solve problems and improve as you go: you look for solutions before escalating, make decisions within your own tasks, and speak up when you spot a smarter way
Comfortable in corporate English (our working language)


Practical details


16-20 hours per week, preferably as two full days, but we're flexible (also around your exam periods)
Start: August or September 2026
Location: Our Copenhagen office is preferred, but working from home is possible when needed
Salary: [per hour, according to qualifications / standard student assistant terms]


How To Apply

Instead of a long cover letter, feel free to tell us in a few lines about why you can help us keep our data in order and improve customer retention. We start reviewing applications in August 2026 and hold interviews on a rolling basis.

Questions? Contact Bjarke B. Kristoffersen, Group Customer Experience & Lifecycle Manager, at Bjarke.kristoffersen@elcg.dk.

European LifeCare Group is committed to providing equal opportunities to all applicants, regardless of race, religion, sex, national origin, age, disabilities, genetic information, marital status, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by law.

European LifeCare Group is one of Europe's largest independent provider of travel clinics and public vaccination programmes.

Founded in 1998, today European LifeCare Group has specialist clinics covering most of Denmark, the Netherlands, Sweden and the UK, where the company trades under a number of brands, including Danske Lægers Vaccinations Service, Vaccination UK, London Travel Clinic, Citydoc, Resecentrum vaccinationer, Mediprevent, Vaccinaties Op Reis and PreventVaccins.

European LifeCare Group also works for both the Danish and English National Health Service as a contracted service provider of immunisation programmes in schools and national influenza vaccination programmes.

Instead of a long cover letter, feel free to tell us in a few lines about why you can help us keep our data in order and improve customer retention. We start reviewing applications in August 2026 and hold interviews on a rolling basis.

Questions? Contact Bjarke B. Kristoffersen, Group Customer Experience & Lifecycle Manager, at Bjarke.kristoffersen@elcg.dk.

European LifeCare Group is committed to providing equal opportunities to all applicants, regardless of race, religion, sex, national origin, age, disabilities, genetic information, marital status, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by law.

European LifeCare Group is one of Europe's largest independent provider of travel clinics and public vaccination programmes.

Founded in 1998, today European LifeCare Group has specialist clinics covering most of Denmark, the Netherlands, Sweden and the UK, where the company trades under a number of brands, including Danske Lægers Vaccinations Service, Vaccination UK, London Travel Clinic, Citydoc, Resecentrum vaccinationer, Mediprevent, Vaccinaties Op Reis and PreventVaccins.

European LifeCare Group also works for both the Danish and English National Health Service as a contracted service provider of immunisation programmes in schools and national influenza vaccination programmes.

Title: People & Culture koordinator med Teamfokus (Barselsvikariat)
Place: Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Er du klar til at blive en del af et dynamisk og udviklingsorienteret team, hvor vi støtter hinanden og skaber resultater sammen? Vil du være med til at forme fremtiden i vores People & Culture-afdeling? Så kan dette være din næste karrieremulighed!

Vi søger en People & Culture-koordinator til et barselsvikariat (tidsbegrænset ansættelse) med opstart den 1. oktober 2026. Der er mulighed for fastansættelse. I rollen bliver du en vigtig del af vores People & Culture-team, hvor du vil have fokus på HR-administration samt support til ledere og medarbejdere på tværs af organisationen.

Din rolle

Som P&C koordinator vil du være en vigtig spiller i at sikre en smidig HR-administration og ensartede processer, der fungerer effektivt i praksis. Du vil arbejde med alt fra udarbejdelse af kontrakter til at sparre med ledere om overenskomster og personaleforhold. Du trives med faste, tilbagevendende opgaver, men også i et miljø præget af forandring, hvor du byder ind med idéer til forbedringer, der styrker vores løbende udvikling.

People & Culture i Forenede

"P&C-teamet består af 10 dedikerede kolleger med forskellige faglige baggrunde og kompetencer. Vi værdsætter forskellighed og er gode til at tale åbent om både opgaverne og de udfordringer, der kan opstå. Vi er produktive, men vi har det også sjovt sammen! Hos os er mennesket i centrum, og vi ved, at succes i hverdagen kræver en stærk teamindsats," fortæller Teamleder Arjeta Ibisi Jusufi.

Hvad vil dine opgaver være?

Vi Er Et Engageret Team, Hvor Vi Støtter Hinanden Og Sikrer, At Opgaverne Bliver Løst - Også Når Én Har Brug For Backup. Dine Primære Ansvarsområder Vil Inkludere


Udarbejdelse af ansættelseskontrakter, tillæg, opsigelser for alle medarbejdertyper
Indhentning og opfølgning på attester herunder arbejds- og opholdstilladelser
Sparring med ledere om overenskomster og HR-relaterede spørgsmål
Håndtering af medarbejderforespørgsler
Administration og vedligeholdelse af vores HR-system
Optimering og implementering af effektive arbejdsgange og processer


Vi har tæt samarbejde med både ledere og medarbejdere i hele virksomheden samt med lønteamet for at sikre korrekt lønudbetaling.

Hvem er du?

For at få succes i rollen er det vigtigt, at du kan levere på følgende områder:


Du er en stærk teamplayer, som trives i samarbejde med andre. Du deler din viden, hjælper dine kolleger, kommunikerer klart og viser ægte interesse for menneskene omkring dig.
Du kan lide at følge processer, dykke ned i detaljer samt sørge for at kontrakterne står skarpt og at HR-systemet er opdateret.
Du har erfaring med overenskomster og er vant til at arbejde med dem i praksis, så du ved, hvad du skal være opmærksom på, når du sparer med lederne.
Du har en baggrund inden for HR-administration og er komfortabel med personalesager samt at give konstruktiv feedback til ledere.
Du behersker dansk i både skrift og tale og kan begå dig på engelsk, både skriftligt og mundtligt.


Hvad tilbyder vi?


Et job med reel indflydelse, fleksibilitet og mulighed for hjemmearbejdsdage.
Et team, der prioriterer både drift og godt socialt samvær.
En virksomhed i kulturel udvikling, hvor vi tror på, at tilfredse medarbejdere er nøglen til succes.


Vores værdier - Ansvar, Engagement og Omtanke - udgør kernen i vores kultur. Vi holder, hvad vi lover, ser muligheder og skaber tryghed gennem vores handlinger.

Derudover tilbyder vi ansættelse jf. HK-overenskomsten for kontoransatte herunder pension- og sundhedsordning. Hos os får du også en lækker kantine, massageordning, mulighed for leje af ferieboliger og ikke mindst "forskelsdage". Stillingen er på fuld tid, og du vil få base på vores hovedkontor i Ballerup.

Er du klar til at gøre en forskel?

Vil du være en del af People & Culture teamet?

Er du blevet nysgerrig på stillingen, eller har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte teamleder Arjeta Ibisi Jusufi på mail aribju@forenede.dk.

Vi modtager ansøgninger løbende, så upload gerne din ansøgning og dit CV allerede i dag. Ansøgningerne vil blive gennemgået i starten af august (uge 32). Vi forventer at indkalde til samtaler i uge 33 og 34, og en eventuel anden samtalerunde forventes afholdt i uge 35.

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!
Show more

Title: Associate
Description:
Heidrick & Struggles is looking to hire an Associate to the Global Technology, AI and Services team in Copenhagen




Heidrick and Struggles, a premier executive search and leadership advisory firm, is looking for an outgoing, ambitious and internationally minded full-time Associate for our office in Copenhagen. You will have a central and independent project management role in our executive search engagements in collaboration with colleagues in both Denmark and globally. You will work closely with business executives from the highest levels and gain knowledge of different industries and functions with a particular focus on technology and AI. This is rare opportunity to become part of the team that supports leading organizations in shaping their strategy, leadership and transformation at a focal point in history as AI becomes an integral part of business.




Who We Are

Heidrick & Struggles is a premier provider of executive search, leadership assessment and development, organization and team effectiveness, and culture shaping services globally. In our more than 60 year history, we have conducted some of the most high-profile C-suite and board-level searches in the world.

Today, we are working with our clients across 29 countries to help them accelerate their performance and respond with agility to new opportunities and challenges in every industry. We continue to raise the bar in serving our clients as trusted leadership advisors, and we are also providing an expanded range of data-driven, tech-enabled tools and platforms across our executive search and consulting businesses.

Managing top talent isn’t just our business. It’s our passion, and we are committed to investing in and developing the careers of our own people.

We look for people with experience from a variety of industries and diverse educational backgrounds who are passionate about partnering with innovative and influential organizations and share our commitment to our purpose – to help our clients change the world, one leadership team at a time.

Additional information on the firm can be found at www.heidrick.com.




What We Do

Executive Search Associates play a critical role in the execution of all facets of the executive recruiting lifecycle, from candidate identification to placement. Working closely with our expert Search Partners & Principals (Consultants) and connecting with team members across all levels of the Search team, Associates learn through hands-on experience, and receive world-class training and mentorship in an apprenticeship-like model. Our Associates assist Consultants in all aspects of the recruitment process from developing the search strategy to managing its execution and also support business development activities. The search process includes working with clients to develop position specifications; interviewing, assessing and building a pipeline of qualified candidates for active searches meeting the client’s requirements; developing the candidate relationship; candidate referencing; and assisting with client relationship management.




While learning the Search industry, Associates invest significant time researching and learning about various industries, executive roles and functions, and do so in collaboration with colleagues across the globe. The Associate role is highly visible with significant responsibility and an excellent, clearly defined career track.




Role Responsibilities




Research & Writing:

Conducting industry specific and client company research, mapping the executive talent landscape to bring market insights to the table.
Supporting Consultants in the development of position specification(s) and a focused search strategy.
Assisting Consultants in preparing business development and candidate assessment documents to present to both prospective and current clients.




Internal and External Communication:

Contacting prospective candidates to elicit interest in client opportunities, obtaining referrals, and/or collecting market intelligence and feedback.
Assisting Consultants in conducting in-depth interviews to assess candidate qualifications against position specifications.
Managing candidate relationships throughout the entire life cycle of the search and keeping the Consultant abreast of candidate updates.




Living Our Values:

Partnering well across a multitude of stakeholders with the aim of developing productive and trusting relationships with colleagues across the firm.
Fostering an environment of collaboration; viewed as a team player.
Acting as a culture carrier; contributes to firm, practice and/or office initiatives.
Demonstrating self-awareness, humility, empathy, and open-mindedness in the workplace.




Ethics & Quality:

Maintaining the accuracy, quality, and integrity of all Search information in our various internal and external research tools.
Maintaining status reports and client ready reports for both internal and external presentation(s) to Consultant and Client.
Managing multiple concurrent projects and completing high quality deliverables on time.
Acting with the highest regard to professionalism, confidentiality, and ethics.
Succeeding and persisting through challenges, motivated to do the right thing.







Basic Role Qualifications

Fluent in both English and Danish, with the ability to communicate professionally and effectively in both languages across written and verbal interactions.
2-3 years’ professional experience from consulting, recruitment, investment banking or similar
At minimum an undergraduate BA/BS degree is required.
Exhibits skill and developing mastery of professional communications, displaying adeptness in writing, articulating, and delivering information with confidence and impact.
Developing the ability to collaborate effectively with external clients and candidates, and beginning to understand techniques to influence and build positive relationships.




Preferred Role Qualifications

Prior experience in full life cycle recruiting in Executive Search, Contingent Search or In-House Talent Acquisition, management consulting or other industry requiring applied sales and research skills strongly preferred.
Experience targeting potential candidates, developing pitch materials and conducting market research.
Proven success simultaneously managing multiple projects and working across teams. Proactive and structured approach to move projects forward and deliver in a timely manner.
Experience identifying market trends and researching information effectively through various channels.
Ability to analyze, organize and synthesize large volumes of information into insights. Keen eye for details and quality consciousness.
Experience working with Salesforce or other CRM.
Demonstrated skill in building and maintaining relationships with a diverse group of stakeholders.

Title: Student Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Would you like to work in a dynamic and international environment, and are you a person with a flair for numbers? There are plenty of possibilities to test and develop your skills in a company with increasing focus on data and analytics. This is an opportunity to gain valuable experience within performance optimisation and digitisation, in the maritime industry.




Job Overview




Navigator Gas manages a fleet of gas tankers, traded worldwide. In an ever-changing world where new regulations and standards challenge the industry, the ability to collect and analyse large quantities of data, and to conclude and act on the findings, is essential for being successful. We need you to assist us in this process.




As a company, we are dedicated and ambitious in the pursuit of ever more sustainable and efficient ways of running our business. You will be given a central role, that will be visible and impactful, and important in achieving our goals for 2027 and beyond. You will work with colleagues in multiple departments and with tasks related to analysis, reports, presentations, data organisation, scripting and various ad hoc jobs.




Among the tasks, you will have the opportunity to work with, are the following:




Develop Advanced Procurement Performance Analytics
Drive Predictive Insights & Trend Indicators
Benchmarking & Performance Transparency
Deep-Dive Analysis of End-to-End Process Efficiency
Map AI development opportunities
Cost & Logistics Optimization Insights
Support Data-Driven Decision Making in Procurement




You will report to our Head of Procurement and have involvement in trans-departmental projects and initiatives relevant for your skills set.




About you:




You are currently studying your Bachelor’s / Master’s degree in engineering, business administration, IT, economics, data science, computer science or other relevant education you cannot wait to get your hands even deeper into the practical analytical side. You are interested in working in a high paced international environment.




You’ll be working with data and IT tools to support our team and help turn analysis into real results.




You’re a good fit if you:




Like working with data and finding insights in numbers
Take pride in generating results by delivering your work at the set deadline without compromising quality
Are curious, tech-savvy, and eager to learn new tools and methods
Demonstrates ownership by following through on tasks and seeking clarification when needed.
Are confident engaging key stakeholders and challenge views effectively.
Know your way around Microsoft Excel, Power BI and other Office tools
Fluent in oral and written English as this is our corporate language




We offer a flexible working week of 10-20 hours, with more hours required during holiday periods. Furthermore, we believe in freedom and flexibility under responsibility so the office hours can be adjusted to accommodate your school schedule.




Application




Please submit your application and CV via the “Apply” button as soon as possible and no later than 2 August 2025. Applications will be reviewed ongoingly, and we will conduct interviews in the beginning of August.




Start date for the position will be September 2026.




Navigator Gas is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Prospective employees will receive consideration without discrimination because of race, colour, religion, creed, gender, national origin, age, disability, marital or veteran status, sexual orientation, genetic information, citizenship or any other legally protected status.




We kindly ask you to not have a picture on your CV as we wish to encourage an unbiased recruitment process.

Title: AVP Data Science
Description:
About LoopMe

LoopMe is an AI company solving one of advertising's hardest problems: making brand advertising actually measurable — and making it perform. Our platform runs patented machine learning models across billions of consumer signals in real time, optimising campaigns toward outcomes like purchase intent, brand lift, and foot traffic rather than proxy metrics like clicks. The result is 2-5x better performance than industry benchmarks, at scale.

We operate a high-load programmatic infrastructure — processing millions of ad requests per second with sub-200ms response times globally. This isn't a layer on top of someone else's stack; it's built from the ground up, in-house, by the team you'd be joining. Founded in 2012 and headquartered in London, we now have 400+ people across 19 cities and have sustained 40% revenue CAGR since 2018. The engineering problems here are real, the ownership is genuine, and the scale is significant.


The opportunity

You own LoopMe's AI throttling system and lead AI products across the business — a senior role combining technical ownership, product ownership, and commercial partnership. You lead a team of four within LoopMe's 17-person Data Science organisation, working directly with commercial, operations, product, and engineering teams and reporting measurable impact to the SLT. The wider Data Science team — led by Chief Data Scientist Dr Leonard Newnham — operates as a single team across London, Poland, and Ukraine, with a track record of publishing award-winning research in automated bidding. This role reports to the Chief Data Scientist.


What you'll do

Own LoopMe's AI throttling system end to end, covering roadmap, modelling strategy, release planning, measurement and production performance
Lead and develop a team of four data scientists and ML engineers, setting priorities and maintaining high technical standards
Own the full product lifecycle for AI products, from opportunity identification through launch, monitoring and iteration
Partner with commercial teams and clients to understand their needs and coordinate releases with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Partner with operations teams to embed AI products safely and measurably into day-to-day business processes
Establish clear, evidence-led reporting on progress, risks and measurable commercial impact for senior leadership
Develop practical business recommendations by turning ambiguous commercial problems into testable data science questions
Manage technical quality by reviewing model logic, system design and code where needed

What you'll bring

Essential

About LoopMe

LoopMe is an AI company solving one of advertising's hardest problems: making brand advertising actually measurable — and making it perform. Our platform runs patented machine learning models across billions of consumer signals in real time, optimising campaigns toward outcomes like purchase intent, brand lift, and foot traffic rather than proxy metrics like clicks. The result is 2-5x better performance than industry benchmarks, at scale.

We operate a high-load programmatic infrastructure — processing millions of ad requests per second with sub-200ms response times globally. This isn't a layer on top of someone else's stack; it's built from the ground up, in-house, by the team you'd be joining. Founded in 2012 and headquartered in London, we now have 400+ people across 19 cities and have sustained 40% revenue CAGR since 2018. The engineering problems here are real, the ownership is genuine, and the scale is significant.

The opportunity

You own LoopMe's AI throttling system and lead AI products across the business — a senior role combining technical ownership, product ownership, and commercial partnership. You lead a team of four within LoopMe's 17-person Data Science organisation, working directly with commercial, operations, product, and engineering teams and reporting measurable impact to the SLT. The wider Data Science team — led by Chief Data Scientist Dr Leonard Newnham — operates as a single team across London, Poland, and Ukraine, with a track record of publishing award-winning research in automated bidding. This role reports to the Chief Data Scientist.

What you'll do

  • Own LoopMe's AI throttling system end to end, covering roadmap, modelling strategy, release planning, measurement and production performance
  • Lead and develop a team of four data scientists and ML engineers, setting priorities and maintaining high technical standards
  • Own the full product lifecycle for AI products, from opportunity identification through launch, monitoring and iteration
  • Partner with commercial teams and clients to understand their needs and coordinate releases with technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Partner with operations teams to embed AI products safely and measurably into day-to-day business processes
  • Establish clear, evidence-led reporting on progress, risks and measurable commercial impact for senior leadership
  • Develop practical business recommendations by turning ambiguous commercial problems into testable data science questions
  • Manage technical quality by reviewing model logic, system design and code where needed

What you'll bring

Essential

  • Strong commercial experience in data science, machine learning or applied AI, ideally including production systems at significant scale in an adtech environment
  • A track record of owning AI/ML products end to end, from initial idea through production release and measured business impact
  • Experience leading, coaching or managing data scientists, ML engineers or closely related technical teams
  • Deep understanding of experimentation, causal measurement, model monitoring and the practical realities of production ML
  • Strong Python, SQL and data engineering literacy, with the ability to go deep technically when required

Nice to have

  • Experience with adtech, real-time bidding, DSPs, SSPs, throttling, pacing or auction systems
  • Excellent communication skills, able to explain complex technical systems to commercial, operations and senior leadership teams
  • Experience with Google Cloud, Docker, Kafka, Spark, Airflow, ElasticSearch, ClickHouse or similar production infrastructure
  • Experience owning product roadmaps, release governance or cross-functional delivery across distributed teams and time zones
What we can offer

  • Bonus
  • Hybrid working; meaning you'll spend 3 days a week in our Farringdon office
  • 25 days annual leave, plus the Bank Holidays
  • 1 month work-from-anywhere
  • Annual Wellness Day
  • Health Shield; a cash-back health plan for things like dental, optical, physio and wellbeing
  • Access to Thrive; accessible mental health support all in one app

  • Pension
